commands.review_summary:
Adds an internal code-mode namespace registry and QuickJS namespace bridge with docs, tests, live repro scripts, plugin rollback cleanup, Swift protocol regeneration, codex-supervisor fixes, session-key scoping, and shrinkwrap cleanup.

PR surface: Source +564, Tests +505, Docs +44, Other +770. Total +1883 across 24 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a feature PR rather than a bug report. The PR body does include after-patch local and remote live proof for the new namespace behavior.

target.title: feat: add internal code mode namespaces
target.body:
## Summary

- Add an internal code-mode namespace registry and QuickJS bridge so host-owned namespace globals can be exposed inside `exec` without adding model-visible tools.
- Gate namespaces by run catalog ownership, serialize only safe enumerable scope values, reject forged/unsafe paths, preserve namespace runtime across `wait`, and clear namespace registrations on plugin rollback.
- Document the internal namespace boundary and explicitly keep a public plugin SDK namespace API out of this PR until it can be loader-owned and cache-safe.
- Add a live OpenAI benchmark harness for a fake Fictions service, plus a standalone live Docker repro script for the namespace mode.
- Tighten the exec prompt so models know namespace calls must `return` a final value.

## Verification

- `fnm exec --using 24.15.0 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/code-mode.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (44 passed)
- `fnm exec --using 24.15.0 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.plugin-sdk.config.ts src/plugin-sdk/api-baseline.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (1 passed)
- `fnm exec --using 24.15.0 pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `env OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=0 fnm exec --using 24.15.0 pnpm check:changed`
- `fnm exec --using 24.15.0 pnpm build`
- `node --import tsx scripts/repro/code-mode-namespace-live.ts --tasks=3` (regular 3/3, code-namespace 3/3)
- `pnpm crabbox:run -- --allow-env OPENAI_API_KEY --idle-timeout 45m --ttl 90m --timing-json --shell -- 'node --import tsx scripts/repro/code-mode-namespace-live.ts --tasks=3'` (lease `cbx_6a216056aab1`, run `run_909ea916a62f`, regular 3/3, code-namespace 3/3)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/code-mode.test.ts` (90 passed)
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/code-mode.ts src/agents/code-mode.test.ts scripts/repro/code-mode-namespace-live.ts`
- `bash -n scripts/repro/code-mode-namespace-live-docker.sh scripts/repro/code-mode-namespace-live-scenario.sh`
- `node --check scripts/check-docker-e2e-boundaries.mjs && node --check scripts/lib/docker-e2e-scenarios.mjs && node scripts/check-docker-e2e-boundaries.mjs`
- `CI=1 node scripts/check-docker-e2e-boundaries.mjs && CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run test/scripts/docker-e2e-plan.test.ts` (remote run `run_32d32d42fb03`, 26 passed)
- `OPENCLAW_CODE_MODE_LIVE_TASKS=1 node --import tsx scripts/repro/code-mode-namespace-live.ts` (regular 1/1, code-namespace 1/1)
- `OPENCLAW_CODE_MODE_LIVE_TASKS=3 OPENCLAW_DOCKER_BUILD_ON_MISSING=1 pnpm test:docker:live-code-mode` (remote run `run_f5633d35f8e0`, regular 3/3, code-namespace 3/3; this was before moving the Docker wrapper out of `scripts/e2e`)
- `OPENCLAW_CODE_MODE_LIVE_TASKS=1 OPENCLAW_DOCKER_BUILD_ON_MISSING=1 pnpm test:docker:live-code-mode` (remote run `run_4ae68a8c9b13`, regular 1/1, code-namespace 1/1; the temporary npm alias executed `bash scripts/repro/code-mode-namespace-live-docker.sh` and was removed afterward to keep dependency guard out of this PR)
- `CI=1 node --import tsx scripts/check-madge-import-cycles.ts && CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/code-mode.test.ts` (remote run `run_3e82078ddc9d`, Madge 0 cycles, 90 passed)
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm deps:shrinkwrap:check` (after rebase onto current `origin/main`)
- `pnpm check:test-types` (after rebase onto current `origin/main`)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.wizard.config.ts --reporter=verbose` (87 passed)
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/code-mode.ts src/agents/code-mode.test.ts src/agents/code-mode-namespaces.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts src/wizard/setup.official-plugins.test.ts scripts/repro/code-mode-namespace-live.ts`
- `node --import tsx scripts/check-madge-import-cycles.ts` (0 cycles)
- `fnm exec --using 24.15.0 pnpm check:test-types` (after final rebase)
- `fnm exec --using 24.15.0 pnpm tsgo:extensions && node scripts/check-extension-package-tsc-boundary.mjs --mode=compile` (110 plugin package-boundary compiles passed)
- `pnpm protocol:check` (after Swift protocol generator refresh)
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` (clean: no accepted/actionable findings after final rebase cleanup)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: code mode can expose internal host-owned namespace globals inside QuickJS while keeping normal tools hidden behind `exec` and `wait`; live model runs can use that namespace surface to inspect and mutate a service. The Docker script is an explicit source-level live repro, not a cataloged release/package Docker E2E lane.

Real environment tested: local macOS checkout with Node 24.15.0; AWS c7a.8xlarge remote runs `run_909ea916a62f`, `run_f5633d35f8e0`, `run_4ae68a8c9b13`, `run_3e82078ddc9d`; OpenAI Responses API with `gpt-5.4-mini` and a real `OPENAI_API_KEY` passed through remote env forwarding.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: targeted code-mode Vitest, plugin SDK API baseline test, plugin SDK API check, local changed gate and build from the original namespace patch; after the live harness addition, ran local and remote Fictions benchmarks, focused code-mode Vitest, targeted oxlint, Docker wrapper syntax/catalog guards, remote Docker repro, Madge cycle proof, whitespace check, and final autoreview as listed above.

Evidence after fix: local live benchmark passed 6/6 task-mode pairs: regular 3/3 with 10,758 ms, 9 model turns, 7 top-level tool calls, 7 service calls; code-namespace 3/3 with 17,248 ms, 9 model turns, 6 top-level tool calls, 4 service calls. Remote live benchmark passed 6/6 task-mode pairs: regular 3/3 with 12,228 ms, 9 model turns, 7 top-level tool calls, 7 service calls; code-namespace 3/3 with 7,728 ms, 9 model turns, 6 top-level tool calls, 4 service calls. The post-move live Docker smoke passed 2/2 task-mode pairs: regular 1/1 with 2,815 ms and 3 service calls; code-namespace 1/1 with 2,287 ms and 1 service call.

Observed result after fix: namespace globals are visible only when the owning plugin id has the required catalog tools in the run, same-named tools from other owners do not expose the namespace, forged namespace paths are rejected, namespace calls survive suspend/resume, and the live Fictions namespace completed risk audit, status promotion, and invoice aggregation tasks with fewer service calls than regular mode.

What was not tested: no real third-party plugin namespace integration, because this PR intentionally does not add a public plugin SDK namespace API. A direct post-alias remote retry of `bash scripts/repro/code-mode-namespace-live-docker.sh` lost SSH during package build before the wrapper reached the test; the same wrapper path had already passed in `run_4ae68a8c9b13`. A repeat broad `pnpm check:changed` after the live harness commit did not complete because local `tsgolint` crashed with a Go SIGSEGV and a Blacksmith Testbox rerun stalled on its `Run Testbox` step; targeted lint/test/live proof passed after that.
